Name: Surskit
Effects: Heal 20 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It appears as if it is skating on water. It draws prey with a sweet scent from the tip of its head.

Name: Masquerain
Ability-Effect: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may put a Pokémon Tool card attached to 1 of your Pokémon into your hand.
Flavor-Text: Its antennae have eye patterns on them. Its four wings enable it to hover and fly in any direction.

Name: Lileep
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if this Pokémon is in your discard pile, you may put this Pokémon on the bottom of your deck.
Effects: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It lived on the seafloor 100 million years ago and was reanimated scientifically.

Name: Cradily
Effects: For each Energy attached to this Pokémon, search your deck for a Stage 2 Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It lives in the shallows of warm seas. When the tide goes out, it digs up prey from beaches.

Name: Tropius
Effects: Draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.
Does 20 more damage for each Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It flies by flapping its broad leaves and gives the sweet, delicious fruit around its neck to children.

Name: Karrablast
Flavor-Text: For some reason they evolve when they receive electrical energy while they are attacking Shelmet.

Name: Shelmet
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: When it and Karrablast are together, and both receive electrical stimulation, they both evolve.

Name: Accelgor
Effects: If an Escavalier you had in play was Knocked Out by damage from an opponent’s attack during his or her last turn, put all Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon into your opponent’s hand.
The Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: When its body dries out, it weakens. So it wraps a membrane around itself for protection while it spits poison.

Name: Virizion-EX
Ability-Effect: Each of your Pokémon that has any [G] Energy attached to it can’t be affected by any Special Conditions. (Remove any Special Conditions affecting those Pokémon.)
Effects: You may search your deck for 2 [G] Energy cards and attach them to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Genesect
Effects: Search your deck for 2 [G] Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon existed 300 million years ago. Team Plasma altered it and attached a cannon to its back.

Name: Genesect-EX
Ability-Effect: When you attach a Plasma Energy from your hand to this Pokémon, you may switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon with his or her Active Pokémon.
Effects: Does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Larvesta
Effects: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Said to have been born from the sun, it spews fire from its horns and encases itself in a cocoon of fire when it evolves.

Name: Volcarona
Effects: Reveal the top 5 cards of your deck and put all Team Plasma cards you find there into your hand. Discard the other cards.
Heal from this Pokémon the same amount of damage you did to the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Thought to be an embodiment of the sun, it appeared during a bitterly cold winter and saved Pokémon from freezing.

Name: Squirtle
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It shelters itself in its shell then strikes back with spouts of water at every opportunity.

Name: Wartortle
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: It is said to live 10,000 years. Its furry tail is popular as a symbol of longevity.

Name: Blastoise
Ability-Effect: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may attach a [W] Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon.
Effects: Does 10 more damage for each [W] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The jets of water it spouts from the rocket cannons on its shell can punch through thick steel.

Name: Lapras
Effects: Heal 30 damage from each of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Able to understand human speech and very intelligent, it loves to swim in the sea with people on its back.

Name: Remoraid
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: The water they shoot from their mouths can hit moving prey from more than 300 feet away.

Name: Octillery
Effects: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It has a tendency to want to be in holes. It prefers rock crags or pots and sprays ink from them before attacking.

Name: Suicune
Ability-Effect: Prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon by Pokémon-EX.
Flavor-Text: It races around the world to purify fouled water. It dashes away with the north wind.

Name: Snorunt
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: It is said that several Snorunt gather under giant leaves and live together in harmony.

Name: Glalie
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Move a [W] Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It prevents prey from escaping by instantaneously freezing moisture in the air.

Name: Froslass
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, your opponent can’t attach any Special Energy cards from his or her hand to his or her Pokémon.
Effects: Does 10 damage to each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It freezes prey by blowing its -58 degrees F breath. It is said to then secretly display its prey.

Name: Relicanth
Effects: Put 2 Item cards that have Fossil in their names from your discard pile into your hand.
Flavor-Text: A rare Pokémon discovered during a deep-sea exploration. It has not changed in over 100 million years.

Name: Snover
Flavor-Text: Seemingly curious about people, they gather around footsteps they find on snowy mountains.

Name: Abomasnow
Effects: Both this Pokémon and the Defending Pokémon are now Confused.
Flavor-Text: They appear when the snow flowers bloom. When the petals fall, they retreat to places unknown again.

Name: Tirtouga
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if this Pokémon is in your discard pile, you may put this Pokémon on the bottom of your deck.
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon was restored from a fossil. It swam skillfully and dove to depths beyond half a mile.

Name: Carracosta
Effects: You may discard an Item card that has Fossil in its name from your hand. If you do, this attack does 50 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It could knock out a foe with a slap from one of its developed front appendages and chew it up, shell or bones and all.

Name: Ducklett
Effects: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: They are better at swimming than flying, and they happily eat their favorite food, peat moss, as they dive underwater.

Name: Kyurem-EX
Effects: Does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
Discard 2 [W] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Tynamo
Flavor-Text: One alone can emit only a trickle of electricity, so a group of them gathers to unleash a powerful electric shock.

Name: Eelektrik
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It wraps itself around its prey and paralyzes it with electricity from the round spots on its sides. Then it chomps.

Name: Eelektross
Effects: Discard as many Energy attached to your Pokémon as you like. This attack does 30 damage times the number of Energy cards you discarded.
Flip 4 coins. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: It latches on to prey with its sucker mouth, sinking in its fangs and shocking the prey with powerful electricity.

Name: Drifloon
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
This attack does 40 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: These Pokémon are called the “Signpost for Wandering Spirits.” Children holding them sometimes vanish.

Name: Drifblim
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon’s attacks cost [C] less for each of your opponent’s Team Plasma Pokémon in play.
Effects: Discard a Special Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: They carry people and Pokémon, but the wind can catch them, so there can’t be a fixed destination.

Name: Uxie
Effects: Put 3 damage counters on your opponent’s Pokémon in any way you like.
Flavor-Text: When Uxie flew, people gained the ability to solve problems. It was the birth of knowledge.

Name: Mesprit
Ability-Effect: If you have Uxie and Azelf in play, each of your Pokémon has no Weakness.
Flavor-Text: When Mesprit flew, people learned the joy and sadness of living. It was the birth of emotions.

Name: Azelf
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Move as many Special Energy attached to your opponent’s Pokémon to your opponent’s other Pokémon in any way you like.
Flavor-Text: When Azelf flew, people gained the determination to do things. It was the birth of willpower.

Name: Munna
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon appears before people and Pokémon who are having nightmares and eats those dreams.

Name: Musharna
Effects: Draw 3 cards. This Pokémon is now Asleep.
The Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: The dream mist coming from its forehead changes into many different colors depending on the dream that was eaten.

Name: Sigilyph
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon may have up to 4 Pokémon Tool cards attached to it. (If this Pokémon loses this Ability, discard Pokémon Tool cards attached to this Pokémon until only 1 Pokémon Tool card remains.)
Flavor-Text: The guardians of an ancient city, they always fly the same route while keeping watch for invaders.

Name: Solosis
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: Because their bodies are enveloped in a special liquid, they are fine in any environment, no matter how severe.

Name: Duosion
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: When their two divided brains think the same thoughts, their psychic power is maximized.

Name: Reuniclus
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 30 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: They use psychic power to control their arms, which are made of a special liquid. They can crush boulders psychically.

Name: Golett
Flavor-Text: Ancient science fashioned this Pokémon from clay. It’s been active for thousands of years.

Name: Golurk
Effects: If you have any Team Plasma Pokémon in play, this attack does nothing.
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.
Flavor-Text: Golurk were created to protect people and Pokémon. They run on a mysterious energy.

Name: Machop
Flavor-Text: Though small in stature, it is powerful enough to easily heft and throw a number of Geodude at once.

Name: Machoke
Effects: If this Pokémon’s remaining HP is 10, this attack does 70 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It happily carries heavy cargo to toughen up. It willingly does hard work for people.

Name: Machamp
Ability-Effect: The damage of each of your [F] Pokémon’s attacks isn’t affected by Resistance.
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, during your opponent’s next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is increased by 30 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: Its four muscled arms slam foes with powerful punches and chops at blinding speed.

Name: Machamp
Effects: Discard a random card from your opponent’s hand.
If this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool card attached to it, this attack does 40 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its four muscled arms slam foes with powerful punches and chops at blinding speed.

Name: Throh
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
Does 80 damage minus 20 damage for each [C] in the Defending Pokémon’s Retreat Cost.
Flavor-Text: When it encounters a foe bigger than itself, it wants to throw it. It changes belts as it gets stronger.

Name: Sawk
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon is a Team Plasma Pokémon, this attack does 40 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Desiring the strongest karate chop, they seclude themselves in mountains and train without sleeping.

Name: Archen
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if this Pokémon is in your discard pile, you may put this Pokémon on the bottom of your deck.
Flavor-Text: It was revived from an ancient fossil. Not able to fly, it lived in treetops and hopped from one branch to another.

Name: Archeops
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 more damage for each heads.
If this Pokémon’s remaining HP is 50 or less, this attack’s base damage is 50.
Flavor-Text: It runs better than it flies. It takes off into the sky by running at a speed of 25 mph.

Name: Houndour
Effects: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It is smart enough to hunt in packs. It uses a variety of cries for communicating with others.

Name: Houndoom
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
If the Defending Pokémon is a Team Plasma Pokémon, this attack does 60 more damage, and the Defending Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: The flames it breathes when angry contain toxins. If they cause a burn, it will hurt forever.

Name: Aron
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: In order to build up its steel body, it eats iron ore. This pesky Pokémon is known to eat railroad tracks.

Name: Lairon
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: Lairon fight over territory, and when their steel bodies collide, sparks fly.

Name: Aggron
Effects: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
Does 20 damage to each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent’s). (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It claims an entire mountain as its own. The more wounds it has, the more it has battled, so don’t take it lightly.

Name: Jirachi-EX
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench, you may search your deck for a Supporter card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Effects: Both this Pokémon and the Defending Pokémon are now Asleep.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Escavalier
Effects: Does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
This Pokémon can’t use Slashing Strike during your next turn.
Flavor-Text: Wearing the shell covering they stole from Shelmet, they defend themselves and attack with two lances.

Name: Bagon
Flavor-Text: Dreaming of one day flying, it practices by leaping off cliffs every day.

Name: Shelgon
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Within its rugged shell, its cells have begun changing. The shell peels off the instant it evolves.

Name: Salamence
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may discard all Pokémon Tool cards attached to each of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Effects: Discard any Stadium card in play.
Flavor-Text: As a result of its long-held dream of flying, its cellular structure changed, and wings grew out.

Name: Dialga-EX
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, put a card from your discard pile into your hand.
For each Plasma Energy attached to this Pokémon, discard the top card of your opponent’s deck.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Palkia-EX
Effects: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Heal from this Pokémon 20 damage for each Plasma Energy attached to this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Axew
Effects: Search your deck for a Supporter card named Iris, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: Its large tusks have a tendency to break, but each time they grow back, they grow in harder and sturdier.

Name: Fraxure
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: A broken tusk will not grow back, so it diligently sharpens its tusks on river rocks after the end of a battle.

Name: Haxorus
Effects: Does 40 damage times the amount of [M] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
If the Defending Pokémon is a Team Plasma Pokémon, it is Knocked Out. (If the Defending Pokémon is not a Team Plasma Pokémon, this attack does nothing.)
Flavor-Text: Its tusks are incredibly destructive. They can easily slice through a thick, sturdy steel column every time.

Name: Druddigon
Effects: Flip 2 coins. If either of them is tails, this attack does nothing.
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by any effects on the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It runs through the narrow tunnels formed by Excadrill and Onix. It uses its sharp claws to catch prey.

Name: Kangaskhan
Effects: Search your deck for 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flip 4 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: It raises its offspring in its belly pouch. It lets the baby out to play only when it feels safe.

Name: Porygon
Flavor-Text: A man-made Pokémon created using advanced scientific means. It can move freely in cyberspace.

Name: Porygon2
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It was upgraded to enable the exploration of other planets. However, it failed to measure up.

Name: Porygon-Z
Ability-Effect: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may move a Plasma Energy attached to 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon.
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: Its programming was modified to enable work in alien dimensions. It did not work as planned.

Name: Teddiursa
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: It lets honey soak into its paws so it can lick them all the time. Every set of paws tastes unique.

Name: Ursaring
Effects: During your next turn, each of this Pokémon’s attacks does 50 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: In its territory, it leaves scratches on trees that bear delicious berries or fruits.

Name: Chatot
Effects: Discard all Pokémon Tool cards attached to each of your opponent’s Pokémon.
The Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: Its tongue is just like a human’s. As a result, it can cleverly mimic human speech.

Name: Caitlin
Description: Put as many cards from your hand as you like on the bottom of your deck in any order. Then, draw a card for each card you put on the bottom of your deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Cover Fossil
Description: Look at the bottom 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal a Tirtouga you find there and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Energy Retrieval
Description: Put 2 basic Energy cards from your discard pile into your hand.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Iris
Description: During this turn, your Pokémon’s attacks do 10 more damage to the Active Pokémon for each Prize card your opponent has taken (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Plume Fossil
Description: Look at the bottom 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal an Archen you find there and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Pokémon Catcher
Description: Switch your opponent’s Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Professor Juniper
Description: Discard your hand and draw 7 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Rare Candy
Description: Choose 1 of your Basic Pokémon in play. If you have a Stage 2 card in your hand that evolves from that Pokémon, put that card on the Basic Pokémon. (This counts as evolving that Pokémon.) You can’t use this card during your first turn or on a Basic Pokémon that was put into play this turn.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Reversal Trigger
Description: When the Team Plasma Pokémon this card is attached to is Knocked Out by damage from an opponent’s attack, search your deck for a card and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Name: Root Fossil Lileep
Description: Look at the bottom 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal a Lileep you find there and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Silver Bangle
Description: The attacks of the Pokémon this card is attached to (excluding Pokémon-EX) do 30 more damage to Active Pokémon-EX (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Name: Silver Mirror
Description: Prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to the Pokémon this card is attached to (excluding Pokémon-EX) by your opponent’s Team Plasma Pokémon.
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Name: Ultra Ball
Description: Discard 2 cards from your hand. (If you can’t discard 2 cards, you can’t play this card.) Search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Plasma Energy
Description: This card provides [C] Energy.

Name: G Booster
Effects: Discard 2 Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack’s damage isn’t affected by any effects on the Defending Pokémon.
Description: The Genesect-EX this card is attached to can also use the attack on this card. (You still need the necessary Energy to use this attack.)
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Name: G Scope
Effects: This attack does 100 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Description: The Genesect-EX this card is attached to can also use the attack on this card. (You still need the necessary Energy to use this attack.)
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Name: Master Ball
Description: Search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Scoop Up Cyclone
Description: Put 1 of your Pokémon and all cards attached to it into your hand.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Virizion-EX
Ability-Effect: Each of your Pokémon that has any [G] Energy attached to it can’t be affected by any Special Conditions. (Remove any Special Conditions affecting those Pokémon.)
Effects: You may search your deck for 2 [G] Energy cards and attach them to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Genesect-EX
Ability-Effect: When you attach a Plasma Energy from your hand to this Pokémon, you may switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon with his or her Active Pokémon.
Effects: Does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Jirachi-EX
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench, you may search your deck for a Supporter card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Effects: Both this Pokémon and the Defending Pokémon are now Asleep.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Dialga-EX
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, put a card from your discard pile into your hand.
For each Plasma Energy attached to this Pokémon, discard the top card of your opponent’s deck.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Palkia-EX
Effects: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Heal from this Pokémon 20 damage for each Plasma Energy attached to this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Iris
Description: During this turn, your Pokémon’s attacks do 10 more damage to the Active Pokémon for each Prize card your opponent has taken (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Exeggcute
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if this Pokémon is in your discard pile, you may put this Pokémon into your hand.
Flavor-Text: This extremely rare Pokémon is a different color than usual. It is very hard to find.

Name: Virizion
Effects: Draw 2 cards.
During your next turn, this Pokémon’s Leaf Wallop does 40 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: This extremely rare Pokémon is a different color than usual. It is very hard to find.

Name: Dusknoir
Ability-Effect: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may move 1 damage counter from 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon to another of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.
Flavor-Text: This extremely rare Pokémon is a different color than usual. It is very hard to find.

Name: Rare Candy
Description: Choose 1 of your Basic Pokémon in play. If you have a Stage 2 card in your hand that evolves from that Pokémon, put that card on the Basic Pokémon. (This counts as evolving that Pokémon.) You can’t use this card during your first turn or on a Basic Pokémon that was put into play this turn.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).
